🎯 PrepMate — AI-Powered Interview Preparation Platform
Practice smarter. Speak your answers. Get instant AI feedback.

React TypeScript Gemini AI Render

🚀 Live Demo · ✨ Features · ⚡ Quick Start · 📖 Documentation

🔗 Live App: https://prepmate-lvyv.onrender.com/

PrepMate is an AI-powered mock interview platform that generates real interview questions with Google Gemini AI, lets you answer by voice, evaluates your responses instantly, and tracks your progress with visual analytics — all wrapped in a fast, modern, secure web app.


🎯 Problem It Solves

Most candidates prepare for interviews by reading question lists — with no practice speaking answers out loud, no feedback, and no way to track improvement over time.

😩 Problem📋 ExampleNo real practiceReading Q&A PDFs instead of speaking answers aloudNo feedback loopNo idea if an answer is strong, vague, or off-topicNo progress trackingCan't tell if performance is actually improving over timeGeneric question banksStatic questions that don't adapt to a role or difficulty level

PrepMate automates this entire loop — AI-generated questions, spoken answers, instant AI evaluation, and analytics — in one authenticated, persistent platform.


🏗️ Architecture

<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," alt="PrepMate Architecture Diagram" width="100%"/>
Flow: The React client captures voice/text answers and calls the Express/TypeScript backend over authenticated (JWT) REST requests. The backend's Interview Engine generates and scores questions via the Gemini AI client layer, persists sessions to MongoDB (falling back to a local JSON store if unset), and reports health/errors to /api/health and Sentry. The whole stack runs under PM2 in a Docker container, deployed to Render with GitHub Actions handling CI/CD.


✨ Key Features

🤖 AI-Generated Interview Questions


Dynamic question generation powered by the Google Gemini API (@google/genai)
Offline/fallback question bank when no API key is configured, so the app always works
Supports different roles/topics for tailored practice sessions


🎙️ Voice-Based Answering


Speak your answers using the browser's microphone instead of typing
Automatic re-request of mic permission if initially denied
"Retry Microphone" recovery flow if access is blocked at the OS/browser level


🧠 Instant AI Evaluation


Answers are analyzed and scored by AI right after submission
Structured feedback so you know what to improve before the next question


📊 Performance Analytics Dashboard


Visual charts (Recharts) tracking scores and progress across sessions
Session history so you can see improvement over time


🔐 Secure Authentication


User accounts protected with JWT-based authentication
Persistent sessions tied to each user


💾 Flexible Data Storage


MongoDB for production-grade persistence
Automatic fallback to a local JSON store when no database is configured — great for local development


⚡ Modern, Fast Frontend


Built with React 19 + Vite for near-instant hot reload
Styled with Tailwind CSS
Smooth UI motion via Framer Motion
Clean iconography via Lucide Icons


🩺 Production-Ready Reliability


Dedicated health check endpoint (/api/health) for uptime monitoring
Sentry integration for real-time error monitoring
PM2 process management for stable production runs


🐳 Containerized & Cloud-Ready


Full Docker + docker-compose setup for local parity with production
Render-ready deployment via render.yaml
GitHub Actions workflow to trigger automatic deploys on push to main


All Features at a Glance

FeatureDescriptionStatusAI Question GenerationGemini-powered dynamic interview questions✅Offline Question FallbackWorks without a Gemini API key✅Voice AnsweringSpeak answers via browser microphone✅Mic Permission RecoveryRetry flow when mic access is denied✅AI Answer EvaluationInstant scoring & feedback on responses✅Performance AnalyticsVisual charts of scores over time✅Session HistoryTrack past mock interviews✅JWT AuthenticationSecure login & protected sessions✅MongoDB PersistenceProduction-grade data storage✅Local JSON Fallback DBZero-config local development✅Health Check Endpoint/api/health for uptime monitoring✅Sentry Error MonitoringReal-time production error tracking✅PM2 Process ManagementStable, production-grade process handling✅Docker SupportContainerized dev & prod environments✅Render DeploymentOne-step deploy via render.yaml✅GitHub Actions CI/CDAuto-deploy to Render on push✅


🛠️ Tech Stack

LayerTechnologyFrontendReact 19, Vite, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Lucide IconsBackendNode.js, Express, TypeScriptAI EngineGoogle Gemini API (@google/genai)DatabaseMongoDB (falls back to local JSON DB if unset)AuthJWTChartsRechartsProcess ManagementPM2MonitoringSentryDeploymentDocker, Render, GitHub Actions


📂 Project Structure

prepmate/
├── .github/workflows/     # CI/CD workflows (Render deploy)
├── assets/images/         # Static image assets
├── data/                  # Local/offline JSON data store
├── scripts/               # Utility & test scripts (e.g. interview flow test)
├── server/                # Backend server modules
├── src/                   # React frontend source
├── server.ts              # Express server entry point
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
├── render.yaml             # Render deployment manifest
└── package.json


⚡ Quick Start

Prerequisites


Node.js (LTS recommended)
npm


1️⃣ Clone & Install

bashgit clone https://github.com/HARINI-MOHAN-KUMAR/prepmate.git
cd prepmate
npm install

2️⃣ Configure Environment

bashcp .env.example .env

VariableRequiredDescriptionGEMINI_API_KEYOptionalGoogle Gemini API key — offline fallback used if omittedMONGODB_URIRecommended (prod)MongoDB connection string — falls back to local JSON DB if unsetJWT_SECRET✅ (prod)Secret used to sign JWTsSENTRY_DSNOptionalSentry DSN for error tracking

3️⃣ Run Locally

bashnpm run dev

Visit http://localhost:5173 (or the port shown in your terminal) to see the app.


🎙️ Voice / Microphone Troubleshooting


If the browser denies microphone access, click Speak Answer — the app re-requests permission automatically.
If the permission prompt doesn't reappear, use the "Retry Microphone" banner button.
If access is still denied, check your browser settings and explicitly allow microphone access for localhost / 127.0.0.1, then reload.



🔧 Development

Hot Module Reload (HMR) Port

Override the default HMR port if it conflicts with something else:

bashVITE_HMR_PORT=24678 VITE_HMR_HOST=127.0.0.1 npm run dev

Automated Interview Flow Test

bash# Terminal 1 — start the server
npm run dev

# Terminal 2 — run the flow test
npm run test:flow

Available Scripts

ScriptDescriptionnpm run devStart the dev server (frontend + backend)npm run buildBuild the frontend (Vite) and bundle the servernpm start / npm run start:prodRun the production buildnpm run test:flowRun the automated interview flow testnpm run lintType-check with tsc --noEmitnpm run cleanRemove the dist build output


🐳 Docker

Build the image:

bashnpm run docker:build

Run with Docker Compose:

bashnpm run docker:up

Stop containers:

bashnpm run docker:down

Production (PM2, without Docker):

bashnpm run build
npm run start:prod


☁️ Deployment

🔗 Live App

The production app is deployed on Render:
https://prepmate-lvyv.onrender.com/

Deploy Your Own to Render

This project ships with a render.yaml manifest for one-step Docker-based deployment.


Push the repository to GitHub.
In Render, create a new service → Connect a repository → select this repo/branch.
Render reads render.yaml and provisions the web service using the included Dockerfile.
Set the following environment variables in the Render dashboard (Environment tab):

JWT_SECRET — required in production
MONGODB_URI — recommended for production
GEMINI_API_KEY — optional (offline fallback used if empty)
SENTRY_DSN — optional



Trigger a manual deploy, or enable automatic deploys on push.



⚠️ Never commit real secrets to the repository — set them via the Render dashboard.



The service exposes a health check endpoint at /api/health for uptime monitoring.

Automatic Deploys via GitHub Actions

A workflow at .github/workflows/render-deploy.yml triggers a Render deploy automatically on every push to main.


In your GitHub repo, go to Settings → Secrets → Actions and add:

RENDER_API_KEY — a Render API key with deploy permissions
RENDER_SERVICE_ID — your Render service ID



Push to main — the workflow calls the Render API to request a new deploy (Render performs the actual build; monitor progress in the Render dashboard).



🌐 API Endpoints (Backend)

MethodEndpointDescriptionGET/api/healthHealth check for uptime monitoringPOST/api/auth/registerRegister a new userPOST/api/auth/loginAuthenticate and receive a JWTPOST/api/interview/startStart a new AI-generated mock interview sessionPOST/api/interview/answerSubmit a (voice or text) answer for AI evaluationGET/api/interview/historyFetch past session results & analytics


Endpoint names may vary slightly depending on your current backend routes — update this table to match server/ if it differs.




🩹 Notes


When MONGODB_URI is not set, the app uses a local JSON database — handy for local development, not recommended for production.
GEMINI_API_KEY is optional; without it, the app falls back to offline question generation and rule-based evaluation.
Set a strong, unique JWT_SECRET in any public deployment.

Contributions, issues, and feature requests are welcome. Feel free to open an issue or submit a pull request.
